title: 1. Sandboxing strategies: Linux namespaces and cgroups

blocks.content: Running untrusted agent code requires isolation. Namespaces partition system resources (network interfaces, mounts, process lists), and cgroups limit compute resource quotas (CPU, memory), preventing malicious loops from exhausting host power.

blocks.code:
# Run agent container with strict memory limits
docker run --memory="512m" --cpus="1.0" --network=none adk-agent-image

blocks.content:
Question 1: What linux mechanism restricts an agent's memory usage limits?
- A) Namespaces
- B) Control Groups (cgroups)
- C) Seccomp filters

Feedback: Option B is correct. cgroups throttle and limit hardware resources.
